For safety, you should not physically install, remove, or adjust the anchor light while the boat is moving. If you will be operating before dawn, after dusk, or in reduced visibility, set the anchor light up before getting underway, then use the light switch from the helm as needed.

<PIC>

Use the NAV/ANC switch as follows:
- NAV / upper side: turns on the bow lights and the all-around anchor light for night operation while underway.
- ANC / lower side: turns on only the anchor light, for when the boat is anchored at night.
- Middle position: turns the lights off.

For 210FSH SPORT:
The anchor light is mounted on top of the T-top. Before moving the boat, pull the lock tab, raise the anchor light, then return the lock tab to its original position so the light is locked upright. Once it is raised and locked, operate it from the NAV/ANC switch at the helm.

<PIC>

For 210FSH DELUXE:
Do not try to install the removable anchor light while the boat is moving. It is stored inside the lockable storage compartment, and that compartment should remain closed while underway. Set it up before departure by removing it from storage, extending the pole, installing it into the anchor light socket, and securing the stopper. After it is installed, use NAV while running at night or ANC only when anchored.

<PIC>

In short: install or raise the anchor light before the boat starts moving; while underway, only operate it using the helm switch.,['Manual09_223', 'Manual09_160', 'Manual09_161']

You can use the jet wash to rinse down the deck and other washable areas after your trip. For safety, have a passenger operate the jet wash while the boat operator remains at the helm, keeps the engine shut-off cord attached to their PFD, and continuously watches for people, docks, objects, and other boats.

Before starting, confirm the jet wash shut-off valve is open. If you see water leaking from the hose fitting or coil hose, or anything does not look right, close the shut-off valve immediately and do not continue using the system until the issue is corrected.

<PIC>

1. Connect the coil hose to the jet wash hose fitting. Push the hose connector fully onto the fitting so it seats securely.

<PIC>

2. Start the engines. The jet wash uses the boat’s system while the engines are running, so keep the operator in position and alert while cleaning.

3. Push the jet wash switch on the helm switch panel.

<PIC>

After pressing the switch, water flow will begin after about 5 seconds. The water flow has 3 levels; press the jet wash switch again to cycle through and adjust the flow level.

<PIC>

The display will show the active jet wash mode, such as “Jet Wash Mode 1,” so you can confirm the system is on and see the selected level.

<PIC>

4. Use the jet wash handle lever to spray water. Pull or move the lever to discharge water, and use the nozzle adjustment to fine-tune the spray pattern as needed.

<PIC>

When rinsing after use, work from higher/cleaner areas toward the deck drains, and avoid spraying directly into electrical components, sockets, switches, air filter areas, or other sensitive openings. On the 210FSH DELUXE, make sure the anchor light socket is closed before using the jet wash so water does not enter the socket.

To stop using the jet wash:

1. Push the jet wash switch to turn the system off.
2. Stop the engines.
3. Disconnect the coil hose by pushing the collar on the hose fitting inward, then pulling the hose away from the fitting.

<PIC>

If you will not use the jet wash for an extended period, close the shut-off valve. If the boat will be stored in freezing temperatures, the jet wash hoses should be drained to help prevent freezing damage.,['Manual09_172', 'Manual09_173', 'Manual09_174', 'Manual09_175', 'Manual09_176', 'Manual09_177', 'Manual09_178']

If you mean the water supply for the Jet Wash system, there are two related controls:

- The **JET WASH switch/button** at the helm starts/stops and adjusts the jet wash water flow during use.
- The **shut-off valve** under the rear platform hatch opens or closes the water supply line. If this valve is closed, the Jet Wash button will not supply water properly.

To turn the Jet Wash water supply valve on or off, follow these steps:

1. **Stop the engines first.** This is important for safety before accessing the rear platform area.
2. **Open the rear platform hatch.**
3. **Remove the inspection cover** to access the shut-off valve.

<PIC>

4. To **turn ON/open the water supply**, turn the shut-off valve **90° clockwise**.

<PIC>

5. To **turn OFF/close the water supply**, turn the shut-off valve **90° counterclockwise**.
6. Reinstall the inspection cover securely.
7. Close the rear platform hatch securely.

After the valve is open, you can use the **JET WASH switch/button** to operate the system. When you press the Jet Wash switch, water supply starts after about **5 seconds**, and pressing the switch can adjust the water flow level.,['Manual09_179', 'Manual09_180']

Sure — the removal method depends on whether your boat is a 210FSH SPORT or a 210FSH DELUXE. Before starting, make sure the boat is stopped, the engines are off, and the bimini fabric is dry if possible. It’s also best to have a second person support the top so it does not fall, scratch the boat, or bend the frame.

### For 210FSH SPORT
The SPORT model has the bimini top mounted to the T-top frame. It can be removed and reinstalled.

<PIC>

To remove it:

1. Go to the starboard side of the bimini top and open the flap.

<PIC>

2. Loosen the ratchet, then loosen the strap that secures the bimini top to the frame. Keep one hand on the fabric/frame area while loosening so the top does not shift suddenly.

<PIC>

3. Once the strap is loose, slowly pull the bimini top toward the bow and remove it from the T-top frame.

4. After removal, fold or roll the fabric neatly and store it in a clean, dry location. Avoid placing heavy items on it, and make sure the ratchet/strap hardware is not left where it can scratch the deck or console.

### For 210FSH DELUXE
The DELUXE model uses a frame-style bimini top. It should be fully collapsed before removing it from the boat.

To remove it:

1. First store the bimini top in the fully collapsed position. In general, this means:
   - Remove the lock pins from the center poles and lower the center poles.
   - Pull the bimini top toward the bow.
   - Install the storage cover.
   - While supporting the bimini top, remove the lock pins from the forward support poles.
   - Lower the bimini top carefully onto the rear deck.

2. After the bimini top is fully collapsed and supported, remove the main pole mounting pins that secure the bimini top to the main pole mounting brackets.

<PIC>

3. Lift the bimini top assembly off the boat. Use two people if possible, especially to avoid twisting the frame or scratching the deck.

4. Store the bimini top in a dry, protected area. Do not store it while wet for long periods, and avoid bending the poles or putting weight on the fabric.

When reinstalling either version, reverse the removal steps and confirm all straps, pins, and mounting points are fully secured before operating the boat.,['Manual09_182', 'Manual09_183', 'Manual09_184', 'Manual09_193']

If you want to use the canopy/bimini top, the installation depends on whether your boat is the 210FSH SPORT or the 210FSH DELUXE.

For the 210FSH SPORT, the bimini top is installed on the T-top frame and can be used as a sunshade or light rain cover over the leaning post area.

<PIC>

To install it:

1. Position the bimini top on the T-top frame. Start from the bow side and slowly pull the bimini top rearward into its normal installed position on the frame. Keep the fabric even on both sides so it does not bunch, twist, or pull off-center.

2. Secure the bimini top with the strap. Once the top is in place, pull the securing strap tight, then tighten the ratchet so the fabric is held firmly against the T-top frame.

<PIC>

3. Check the tension. The canopy should be snug, but do not overtighten it to the point that the fabric or stitching is under excessive strain. Make sure the strap is not twisted and that the ratchet is fully tightened.

4. Close the flap on the starboard side of the bimini top. This flap helps finish and secure the side of the top after the strap and ratchet are tightened.

<PIC>

5. Before using the boat, confirm that the top is seated correctly on the frame, the strap is tight, the ratchet is locked, and the flap is fully closed.

For the 210FSH DELUXE, the bimini top is mounted using poles and mounting pins. If the bimini top has been fully removed from the boat, place it back onto the main pole mounting brackets first, then install the main pole mounting pins securely.

<PIC>

After the main pole mounting pins are installed, set up the bimini top for use as follows:

1. Raise the bimini top from the rear deck.
2. Attach each forward support pole to its forward support pole mounting bracket using the lock pin.
3. Remove the storage cover, unfold the bimini top, and pull the canopy toward the stern.
4. Push each center pole upward into position, then install the lock pin to secure it.
5. Check all pins and poles before operating. The main pole mounting pins, forward support pole lock pins, and center pole lock pins should all be fully inserted and secure.

Important operating note for the 210FSH DELUXE: do not exceed 45 mph / 72 km/h with the bimini top in the up position. Also, if you are trailering the boat, the bimini top should be placed in the fully collapsed position and secured so it cannot move during transport.,['Manual09_182', 'Manual09_184', 'Manual09_183', 'Manual09_193']

To store the bimini top in the upright position, first make sure the boat is not being trailered with the top fully extended or upright. For trailering, the bimini top should be fully collapsed to prevent damage.

Follow these steps:

1. Remove the lock pins, then push the center poles down.
<PIC>

2. Pull the bimini top toward the bow until it reaches the upright storage position.
<PIC>

3. Install the storage cover.

If you'd like, I can also give you the steps for storing the bimini top in the fully collapsed position.,['Manual09_189', 'Manual09_190']

If you need to check the engine oil level while you are already on the water, do it only after you have slowed down and secured the boat. If a “Low Oil Pressure” warning appears, reduce engine speed right away, return toward shore if possible, or maneuver to a safe location before checking the oil. Moor or anchor the boat so it cannot drift while you are working around the engine compartment.

Follow these steps:

1. Stop the boat in a safe, stable location
- Keep clear of traffic, swimmers, docks, and obstacles.
- Moor or anchor the boat securely.
- Bring the remote control levers back to neutral and stop the engine you are checking.

2. Warm the engine before reading the oil level
- Start the engine after confirming the area is safe.
- Let it run at idle for at least 6 minutes.
- If the ambient temperature is 20°C / 68°F or below, idle it for an additional 5 minutes.
- Then stop the engine.

3. Open the engine hood
- Lift the engine hood to access the engines.
- Be careful: engine oil and nearby engine parts can be very hot immediately after stopping the engine.

4. Remove and wipe the dipstick
- Loosen and remove the oil tank filler cap. The dipstick is attached to this cap.
- Wipe the dipstick clean with a clean cloth.

<PIC>

1 Oil tank filler cap / Dipstick

5. Take the oil level reading correctly
- Screw the oil tank filler cap/dipstick back into the filler hole until it stops.
- Remove it again and check the oil level.
- The oil should be between the minimum and maximum marks on the dipstick.

<PIC>

1 Dipstick  2 Maximum level mark  3 Minimum level mark

6. Adjust the oil level if needed
- If the oil level is below the minimum mark, slowly add the recommended 4-stroke engine oil, then recheck.
- Repeat the wipe–install–remove–read process until the level is within the correct range.
- If the oil level is significantly above the maximum mark, do not continue normal operation; have the boat checked by a qualified Boat Dealer.

7. Secure everything before continuing
- Reinstall the oil tank filler cap securely and turn it until it stops.
- Repeat the same check for the other engine.
- Close and secure the engine hood before operating the boat again.

Important: Do not run the engine with too little or too much oil, as either condition can damage the engine. Also make sure no water, dirt, or debris enters the oil filler opening while checking or adding oil.,['Manual09_196', 'Manual09_197']

Your boat has a bilge drainage system that collects water from the storage compartments and routes it to the bilge area under the engine compartment. You can use the bilge pump manually, and it also has an automatic function for excess water.

**How to operate it manually:**

1. At the helm switch panel, locate the **bilge pump switch**.
2. Turn/push the bilge pump switch **ON** to start the pump.
3. Leave it running while water is being discharged.
4. Turn it **OFF** when the bilge water has been drained to a normal level.

<PIC>

**Where the water drains out:**

When the pump is operating, bilge water is discharged through the **bilge pump outlet** on the side of the boat. You can check this outlet to confirm that water is being pumped out.

<PIC>

**Automatic bilge pump function:**

Even if you do not turn the bilge pump switch on, the pump can detect excessive water in the bilge and will automatically pump out most of it through the bilge pump outlet. This automatic function can operate even when the battery switches are in the OFF position.

**Indicator light:**

The bilge pump indicator light comes on while the pump is running. If the pump starts automatically, it will continue operating until most of the bilge water has been drained.

<PIC>

**Important use tips:**

- Check the bilge area regularly, especially after rain, washing the boat, or operating in rough water.
- Do not run the engines at full throttle if there is bilge water remaining in the engine compartment, because water can splash into the engines and cause serious damage.
- Before operating the boat, make sure all drain plugs are tightened securely to prevent water from entering the hull.
- If the bilge pump does not run when switched on, check the related circuit breaker/fuse and have the electrical system inspected if the issue continues.,['Manual09_201', 'Manual09_202', 'Manual09_203']

To check the anchor light switch while you are on the water, first make sure the boat is being operated safely: keep a proper lookout, maintain control of the boat, and if possible have another person visually confirm the lights while the operator stays at the helm. This check is best done at low speed or while stopped in a safe area away from traffic.

Before checking, confirm the anchor light is ready for your model:

- 210FSH DELUXE: install the removable anchor light before testing.
- 210FSH SPORT: raise the anchor light on the T-top and make sure it is locked in place.

Then use the NAV/ANC switch on the helm panel:

<PIC>

1. Push the upper side of the NAV/ANC switch.
   - This is the night running position.
   - Confirm that both the bow light and the anchor light come on.

<PIC>

For the anchor light location, check according to your model:

210FSH DELUXE:

<PIC>

210FSH SPORT:

<PIC>

2. Push the lower side of the NAV/ANC switch.
   - This is the anchor-only position.
   - Confirm that only the anchor light comes on and the bow light is off.
   - Use this position when anchored at night, not while running.

3. Move the switch to the middle position.

Important usage note: when operating before dawn, after dusk, or in reduced visibility, use the upper NAV position so both the bow light and anchor light are illuminated. When anchored in the dark, use the lower ANC position so the anchor light alone remains on.,['Manual09_223', 'Manual09_224', 'Manual09_225', 'Manual09_226']

To start the boat’s engines:

1. Always make sure the boat is launched and used in waters that are free from weeds and debris, and at least 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat.

<PIC>

2. Check the O-ring on the hull drain plug and make sure that the plug is tightened securely before launching the boat.

<PIC>

3. Turn the battery switch to the ON position.

4. Push the blower switch to operate the blowers and ventilate the engine compartment for at least 4 minutes.

<PIC>

5. Attach the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) to your PFD. Install the clip onto the engine shut-off switch by pushing the clip groove over the nut beneath the knob. Be sure the cord is not wrapped around the steering wheel or tangled in the controls.

<PIC>

6. Put the remote control levers in the neutral position. The starter motors will not operate unless the remote control levers are in neutral.

7. Turn the main switch keys to the start position and release it when the engines start. If the engines do not start after 5 seconds of cranking, release the keys. Wait at least 15 seconds before trying to start the engines again.

<PIC>

Notice: Never turn the main switch keys to the start position while the engines are running. Do not crank the starter motors continuously for more than 5 seconds.,['Manual09_233', 'Manual09_234', 'Manual09_235', 'Manual09_236', 'Manual09_237']

To turn the boat, turn the steering wheel in the same direction you wish to travel, right or left.

Steering control depends on the combination of steering wheel position and the amount of throttle. The amount of jet thrust, in addition to the position of the steering wheel, determines how sharply you turn.

A. More throttle produces high thrust, so the boat will turn more sharply.  
<PIC>

B. Less throttle produces low thrust, so the boat will turn more gradually.  
<PIC>

C. Pulling the remote control levers back to idle or neutral position produces only minimum thrust. If you are traveling at speeds above trolling, you will have rapidly decreasing ability to steer without throttle.  
<PIC>

D. If the boat is moving and the remote control levers are in neutral position, or if there is no thrust because the engines are stopped, the boat will go straight even though the steering wheel is turned.

You need throttle to steer.  
<PIC>

Turning left:  
<PIC>

Turning right:  
<PIC>

Safety notes: Do not pull the remote control levers back to idle when trying to steer away from objects — you need throttle to steer. Be sure passengers are holding on before making turns.,['Manual09_239', 'Manual09_240', 'Manual09_241', 'Manual09_242', 'Manual09_243', 'Manual09_244']

When crossing wakes or swells, the goal is to reduce the impact on both passengers and the boat while maintaining enough control to steer safely. Use a controlled speed, choose a suitable crossing angle, and avoid trying to “jump” the wake or wave.

<PIC>

Recommended operation:

1. Slow down before reaching the wake or swell  
   Reduce speed early and smoothly. A lower speed usually reduces the jolt when the hull meets the wake or swell. Avoid sudden throttle changes, especially if passengers are not prepared.

2. Cross at an angle instead of straight on when possible  
   “Quarter” the wake or swell by crossing it at an angle. In many conditions, an angle around 45° gives a smoother ride than hitting the wake directly. This lets the boat climb and descend the wake more gradually.

3. Be ready for the boat to pull or steer away from the wake  
   When you cross at an angle, the wake or swell can push the hull and make the boat try to turn away from it. At about 45°, this effect may be mild. At a very shallow angle, such as around 10°, the sideways steering effect can be much stronger. Keep a firm grip on the steering wheel and be prepared to correct your course.

4. Keep passengers seated and holding on  
   Before crossing, make sure all passengers are seated in designated seating areas, feet on the deck, and holding the handgrips. Crossing wakes or swells can cause sudden movement, especially if the wake is steep or close together with other waves.

5. Maintain enough throttle for steering control  
   This boat uses jet thrust for steering, so avoid pulling the levers fully back to idle if you still need to maneuver. If you must change course while crossing or immediately after crossing, you need some throttle for the boat to respond properly.

6. Avoid sharp or aggressive maneuvers  
   Do not try to jump wakes or waves. Jumping can increase the risk of injury and loss of control. Cross smoothly and defensively, and keep a safe distance from other boats, swimmers, docks, and obstacles.

<PIC>

Also keep in mind:

- A single small swell is usually easier and smoother to cross than a large swell or a wake from another boat.  
- A sharp, steep wake will usually produce a harder jolt than a broad, rounded swell.  
- A series of wakes or swells is more difficult to cross smoothly than one isolated wake, so reduce speed further and stay alert.  
- In rough water, poor visibility, or bad weather, operate cautiously and consider avoiding the area if conditions are beyond your comfort or skill level.

In short: slow down, cross at a controlled angle—often around 45°—keep passengers secure, maintain enough throttle to steer, and adjust your steering as the boat reacts to the wake or swell.,['Manual09_249', 'Manual09_250']

To load the boat back onto the trailer:

1. Disconnect the trailer lights from the towing vehicle.

2. Back the trailer down the ramp as close to 90° to the shoreline as you can. If possible, have a second person act as an observer while standing to the side of the trailer. Stop when the tops of the trailer’s fenders are about 3 inches above the waterline.

3. With the boat moving at the slowest idle speed, guide the boat onto the support rails. Use throttle only if necessary for steering ability.

WARNING: Using too much throttle can cause the boat to jump over the front of the trailer, which can result in injury to the boat operator and bystanders.

4. Make sure the boat is centered on the support rails and is headed straight for the bow stop. Ease the boat forward until the bow rests against the bow stop.

NOTICE: The winch line is not designed to pull the boat onto the trailer.

5. Attach and tighten the winch line.

NOTICE: The winch line should not be the only line securing the bow during trailering. Use the chain along with the winch to secure the boat to the trailer.

<PIC>

6. Pull the trailer up the ramp out of the way of other boaters. Attach the bow and stern tie-downs. Reconnect the trailer lights.

<PIC>

7. Perform the post-operation checks.,['Manual09_255', 'Manual09_256']

Cooling system flushing is essential to prevent the cooling system from clogging up with salt, sand, or dirt.

(1) Connect the garden hose adapter to a garden hose.

<PIC>

1 Garden hose adapter 2 Garden hose

(2) Loosen the flush hose connector cap and remove it. Insert the garden hose adapter into the flush hose connector by pushing and twisting it until it is securely connected.

<PIC>

1 Flush hose connector 2 Flush hose connector cap 3 Garden hose adapter

(3) Connect the garden hose to a water tap.

(4) Make sure that the area around the boat is clear, and then start the engine. Immediately after the engine starts, fully turn the water supply on so that water flows out continually from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet.

NOTICE: Never turn on the water before starting the engine. The water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase causing severe engine damage. Be sure to turn on the water immediately after starting the engine to prevent engine overheating.

(5) Run the engine at a fast idle for 3 to 5 minutes. If the engine stops while flushing, turn the water supply off immediately and perform the procedure again from step 4.

(6) Turn off the water supply, and then drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately pushing the remote control lever up to half throttle and back to idle for 10 to 15 seconds.

(7) Stop the engine.

NOTICE: Never have the water on when the engine is not running. The water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase causing severe engine damage. Do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds after the water supply has been turned off to avoid engine overheating.

(8) Remove the garden hose adapter.

(9) Install and tighten the cap securely.,['Manual09_257', 'Manual09_258']

WARNING: Do not use fuses of a higher amperage than recommended. Substitution with a fuse that has an improper rating can cause extensive electrical system damage and possible fire.

If a fuse is blown, replace it with the proper fuse.

To replace a fuse:

(2) Unfasten the band.

(3) Remove the fuse box from the air filter case by pulling the fuse box toward the bow.
(4) While pushing both sides of the fuse box cover inward, pull the cover toward the bow and remove it.

<PIC>
1 Electronic throttle valve fuse 2 Fuel pump fuse 3 Main relay drive fuse 4 Main fuse 5 Fuse puller 6 Battery fuse 7 Spare fuse 8 Unused fuse

(5) When replacing the accessory fuse and bilge pump fuse, remove the fuse holder. Open the battery compartment to access the accessory fuse and bilge pump fuse.

Here’s how to check the engine oil level on your boat safely and correctly.

Before you start, please note these important precautions:

- Engine oil can be extremely hot right after the engines are shut off. Avoid touching hot oil or getting it on your clothes.
- Do not operate the engine with too much or too little oil, as either condition can damage the engine.
- Keep water, dirt, and debris out of the oil filler opening.
- If checking the oil on land, the engine must be running while cooling water is being supplied to the cooling passages.
- If checking the oil while the boat is in the water, make sure the boat is securely moored so it cannot drift.

Steps to check the engine oil level:

1. With the engine stopped, place the boat in a precisely level position on land, or launch and secure it in the water.
2. Check the surrounding area for safety, then start the engine.
3. Let the engine idle for at least 6 minutes. If the outside temperature is 20°C / 68°F or lower, let it idle for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Stop the engine.
5. Open the engine hood.
6. Remove the oil tank filler cap. The dipstick is attached to this cap. Wipe the dipstick clean with a clean cloth.

<PIC>

In the image above, the oil tank filler cap/dipstick is marked as item 1. Each engine has its own cap and dipstick.

7. Screw the oil tank filler cap back into the filler hole until it stops, then remove it again to read the oil level.
8. Check that the oil level is between the minimum and maximum marks on the dipstick.

<PIC>

In this image:
- 1 = Dipstick
- 2 = Maximum level mark
- 3 = Minimum level mark

9. If the oil level is below the minimum mark, slowly add the correct 4-stroke engine oil, then recheck the level.
10. If the oil level is significantly above the maximum mark, do not continue operating the engine; have it checked by a Boat Dealer.
11. Repeat the wipe, insert, remove, and read process until the oil level is within the proper range.
12. Reinstall the oil tank filler cap securely and turn it until it stops.
13. Repeat the same procedure for the other engine.
14. Close the engine hood securely.

Recommended oil guidance: use 4-stroke engine oil only. Suitable oil types include SAE 10W-30, 10W-40, 20W-40, or 20W-50, with API grade SG, SH, SJ, or SL. The best viscosity depends on the operating temperature in your area.,['Manual09_196', 'Manual09_197']

Yes. This coffee machine has an energy-saving function that controls when the machine powers off automatically after use.

By default, the machine will automatically switch off after 9 minutes of inactivity. To turn it back on, simply press either the Espresso button or the Lungo button.

<PIC>

If you want to switch the machine off manually before the automatic shut-off time, press the Espresso and Lungo buttons at the same time.

<PIC>

To change the automatic power-off setting, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is switched off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds.

<PIC>

2. The Espresso button will blink to show the current automatic power-off setting.

<PIC>

3. Press the Espresso button to cycle through the available settings:
- Press once: automatic power-off after 9 minutes
- Press one more time: automatic power-off after 30 minutes
- Press one more time: deactivate automatic power-off

<PIC>

4. After selecting your preferred setting, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds to confirm and exit the setting mode.

<PIC>

Once this is done, the machine will use the selected energy-saving setting the next time it is operated.,['Manual07_4', 'Manual07_5', 'Manual07_6', 'Manual07_7', 'Manual07_8', 'Manual07_9']

You can change the energy saving / automatic power-off setting from the machine’s button menu. By default, the machine powers off automatically after 9 minutes.

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds. The Espresso button is the smaller cup button.
<PIC>

2. The Espresso button will start blinking. The blinking indicates the currently selected energy saving setting.
<PIC>

3. Press the Espresso button to cycle through the available settings. Each press moves to the next option:
- Press once: automatic power-off after 9 minutes
- Press again: automatic power-off after 30 minutes
- Press again: deactivate automatic power-off

If you keep pressing, the options continue cycling in the same order.
<PIC>

4. Once the setting you want is selected, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds to save and exit the energy saving mode. The Lungo button is the larger cup button.
<PIC>

After exiting, the new energy saving setting will be stored and used automatically.,['Manual07_6', 'Manual07_7', 'Manual07_8', 'Manual07_9']

You can program the coffee machine’s water volume by saving a new amount for either the Espresso button or the Lungo button. Please follow these steps:

1. Turn the machine on by pressing either coffee button, then wait until it is ready. The lights should stop blinking and remain steady.  
<PIC>

2. Fill the water tank with fresh drinking water. Then lift the lever fully, insert a capsule, and close the lever completely.  
<PIC>

3. Place a cup under the coffee outlet. Use a cup large enough for the amount you want to program.  
<PIC>

4. Choose the button you want to program:
   - Espresso button: normally set to about 40 ml / 1.35 oz
   - Lungo button: normally set to about 110 ml / 3.7 oz

   Press and hold the chosen button. Coffee will start flowing while you continue holding it.  
<PIC>

5. When the cup reaches your desired volume, release the button immediately.

6. The new water volume is now saved for that button. The next time you press that same button, the machine will automatically dispense the volume you just programmed.

Tip: If you want to program both Espresso and Lungo volumes, repeat the same steps separately for each button. Make sure the lever stays closed during operation, and do not lift it while coffee is flowing.,['Manual07_24', 'Manual07_25', 'Manual07_26', 'Manual07_27']

To empty the system before a long period of non-use, for frost protection, or before maintenance, please follow these steps. Please note: after the emptying cycle, the machine will be temporarily blocked for about 10 minutes before it can be used again.

1. First, switch the machine off by pressing the Espresso and Lungo buttons at the same time.
<PIC>

2. Remove the water tank, then lift the lever fully to open it. If there is a capsule inside, remove/eject it first.
<PIC>

3. With the water tank removed and the lever still open, press and hold both the Espresso and Lungo buttons together for 3 seconds to start the emptying mode.
<PIC>

4. The two button lights will blink alternately. This indicates the machine has entered emptying mode.
<PIC>

5. Close the lever. The machine will then empty the internal water circuit and switch off automatically when finished. A small amount of water or steam may come out during this process, so please keep the drip tray in place and avoid touching the coffee outlet.
<PIC>

6. Once the machine has switched off, empty and clean the used capsule container and drip tray.

After completing the process, wait around 10 minutes before turning the machine on again. If you are preparing the machine for storage or frost protection, keep the water tank empty and store the machine in a dry place.,['Manual07_28', 'Manual07_29', 'Manual07_30', 'Manual07_31', 'Manual07_32']

To reset your coffee machine to factory settings, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Lungo button for about 5 seconds.
<PIC>

2. The LEDs will blink quickly 3 times. This confirms that the machine has been reset successfully.
<PIC>

3. After the reset, the LEDs will continue blinking normally while the machine heats up. Once the lights stay steady, the machine is ready to use again.
<PIC>

After the reset, the default settings will be restored as follows:
- Espresso: 40 ml / 1.35 oz
- Lungo: 110 ml / 3.7 oz
- Auto power-off: 9 minutes

Tip: If the lights do not blink 3 times, turn the machine off and repeat the process, making sure you hold the Lungo button for the full 5 seconds.,['Manual07_33', 'Manual07_34', 'Manual07_35']

To help your coffee maker last longer, it’s best to do a quick clean after each use and keep moisture or coffee residue from sitting inside the machine.

1. After brewing, remove your cup, then lift and close the lever once. This ejects the used capsule into the used capsule container and helps prevent residue from staying in the capsule area.
<PIC>

2. Empty the used capsule container and the drip tray regularly, ideally after daily use or whenever they begin to fill. Rinse the removable pieces with water, wipe away coffee residue, and let them dry before putting them back.
<PIC>

3. Before wiping the machine, unplug it and allow it to cool. Never immerse the machine, power cord, or any electrical part in water, and do not place the machine or its removable parts in a dishwasher.

4. Wipe the coffee outlet regularly with a soft damp cloth. This helps prevent coffee oils from building up around the outlet and keeps the flow clean and consistent.
<PIC>

5. Avoid harsh cleaning methods. Do not use strong cleaning agents, solvents, sharp tools, hard brushes, abrasive pads, steam cleaners, or pressure cleaners, as these can damage the surfaces or internal parts. For the exterior, use a damp cloth and, if needed, a small amount of mild cleaning agent.

6. Keep the water tank fresh. Use fresh, cold drinking water, replace the water if the machine has not been used for a weekend or similar period, and empty the tank if you will not use the machine for an extended time.

7. Descale the machine periodically. Descaling helps maintain water flow, temperature, pressure, and coffee quality over the life of the machine. The frequency depends on your water hardness and how often you brew; harder water requires more frequent descaling.

Following these habits after use will help reduce coffee residue, mineral buildup, and moisture-related wear, which are the main things that affect long-term performance.,['Manual07_23', 'Manual07_48', 'Manual07_47']

The swim platform is the flat boarding area at the stern/rear of the boat. The platform itself does not fold open as a whole. If you want to “open the swim platform,” there are usually two related operations: opening the rear platform hatch under/within the swim platform area, or pulling out the boarding ladder stored under it.

<PIC>

If you mean opening the rear platform hatch to access the wet storage compartment:

1. Stand at the stern swim platform area and locate the lock handle on the rear platform hatch.
2. Pull the lock handle upward.
3. Turn the lock handle clockwise.
4. Lift/open the rear platform hatch.

<PIC>

After the hatch is open, you will see the wet storage compartment underneath the swim platform area.

<PIC>

To close it:

1. Lower and close the rear platform hatch.
2. Turn the lock handle counterclockwise until the hatch is latched securely.
3. Push the lock handle back down so it sits flush.

Important: Make sure the rear platform hatch is fully closed and latched before starting the engines. The engines will not start if this hatch is not securely closed.

Safety reminder: Do not use or stay around the swim platform while the engines are running. Exhaust gases can collect around the stern area, and jet thrust at the rear can be hazardous.,['Manual09_168', 'Manual09_143', 'Manual09_144']

The livewell is located on the starboard side of the boat at the stern. You can open it from the rear starboard seating/deck area.

<PIC>

In the picture above:
1. Livewell lid  
2. Latch

To open the livewell, pull the latch and lift the livewell lid. Once opened, you will see the livewell compartment inside.

<PIC>

To add water to the livewell:
1. Make sure the boat is in the water and the electrical system/accessory power is available.
2. On the helm switch panel, press the livewell switch to turn on the livewell pump. This will begin supplying water into the livewell.
3. Watch the water level as it fills. When there is enough water for your bait or fish, press the livewell switch again to turn the pump off.
4. If you want to keep the water oxygenated and moving, use the aerator switch. The aerator can circulate/aerate the livewell water after the livewell has been filled.

<PIC>

In the switch-panel picture above:
1. Aerator switch  
2. Livewell switch

A few helpful tips:
- If you need light inside the livewell, press the courtesy light switch to turn on the livewell light.
- Do not leave the livewell pump running longer than needed once the water level is sufficient.
- After use, you can drain the livewell by removing the drain plug at the bottom of the livewell, then reinstall the plug securely before using it again.
- To close the livewell, lower the lid and press it down until it is securely latched.,['Manual09_153', 'Manual09_154', 'Manual09_155']

The maintenance setting screen is part of the boat controller’s multi-function display settings. It shows how many engine operating hours have accumulated since the last maintenance reset, so you can track service timing for both engines separately.

<PIC>

On this screen, you will typically see:

- Port Engine: the running hours since the last maintenance reset for the port-side engine.
- Starboard Engine: the running hours since the last maintenance reset for the starboard-side engine.
- Hour display/bar graph: a visual and numeric indication of accumulated operating time.
- Reset button: used after maintenance has been completed to reset the maintenance-hour counter.
- Back button: returns to the previous settings screen without changing the maintenance-hour data.

After the required maintenance has been completed, you can reset the operating-hour count as follows:

1. Tap the “Reset” button on the maintenance setting screen.

<PIC>

2. A confirmation message will appear. Tap “YES” to confirm and reset the number of operating hours since the last maintenance.

<PIC>

If you do not want to reset the maintenance hours, tap “NO” instead. This will cancel the reset and return you to the maintenance setting screen.

Please reset this screen only after maintenance has actually been performed, so the displayed hours remain useful for tracking the next service interval.,['Manual09_83', 'Manual09_84', 'Manual09_85']

The factory reset screen displays the message “Reset all parameters to factory setting?” in the center. At the bottom, there are two buttons: “Reset” on the left and “Back” on the right.

<PIC>,['Manual09_87']

The trip screen is a page on the multi-function display that shows trip-related operating data, such as engine operation time, fuel usage, fuel economy, distance traveled, and water temperature.

<PIC>

On the screen shown, the menu area displays four items at a time. In this example, it is showing:

- Water: ambient water temperature, shown here as 79.3°F
- Economy: average fuel economy, shown here as 23.0 Avg MPG
- Trip: distance traveled since the last reset, shown here as 262.8 miles
- Used: fuel used since the last reset, shown here as 89.9 gallons

The vertical scrollbar on the right lets you scroll through additional trip items, such as fuel flow, highest speed, average speed, and engine hours. The Reset button at the bottom right can be used to reset the resettable trip data. Items such as water temperature and engine hours are informational and cannot be reset.,['Manual09_69']

To make the boat move forward, first make sure the engines are running and both remote control levers are in the neutral position. Then, with the area ahead clear, move both remote control levers forward together from neutral.

<PIC>

As you move the levers forward, the boat shifts into forward. At the initial forward/TDE range, the boat will begin to move slowly and remain responsive for low-speed handling. If you continue pushing the levers farther forward, the engines accelerate and produce more jet thrust, so the boat will move forward faster.

When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates lift fully up, allowing all jet thrust to flow rearward through the jet thrust nozzles. That rearward thrust is what pushes the boat forward.

<PIC>

For safe operation, apply throttle smoothly rather than abruptly, keep passengers seated and holding on before accelerating, and always scan ahead for swimmers, obstacles, docks, and other boats. Also remember that this jet boat needs throttle for steering response, so avoid pulling the levers back to idle if you need to steer away from an object.,['Manual09_46', 'Manual09_50']

To check whether the steering system is working properly while you are driving, do it in a wide, open area with good visibility, light traffic, and no swimmers or obstacles nearby. Keep a safe speed, have passengers seated and holding on, and keep the engine shut-off cord attached to your PFD.

First, before you get underway, make a quick steering check at the helm. The steering wheel should feel secure, with no looseness or free play in or out, and no excessive free play when turning. Turn the wheel fully left and fully right; it should move smoothly through the entire range without binding or restriction.

<PIC>

If someone can safely observe from the stern while the boat is on land, confirm that both jet thrust nozzles move as the steering wheel turns. When you turn the wheel right, the nozzles should point to starboard/right; when you turn the wheel left, they should point to port/left. The articulating keel should move in the same coordinated direction as the nozzles. There should be no noticeable delay or free play between the steering wheel and the nozzles.

<PIC>

Once you are driving, check steering response gently. At low to moderate speed in open water, turn the wheel slightly left and right and confirm that the boat responds smoothly and predictably in the same direction you are steering. Do not make sharp turns until you are sure the response feels normal.

The key point with this jet-propelled boat is that steering depends on both steering wheel position and throttle. Jet thrust is what turns the boat, so if you pull the remote control levers back to idle while trying to avoid something, steering response will drop quickly. Use controlled throttle when you need the boat to respond.

<PIC>

With more throttle, the jet thrust is stronger, so the boat will turn more sharply. This is useful to understand, but use it carefully and only when passengers are braced and the area is clear.

<PIC>

With less throttle, the jet thrust is lower, so the boat will turn more gradually. This is usually the safer way to make a steering-response check while you are learning the boat’s handling.

<PIC>

If the remote control levers are pulled back to idle or neutral, only minimum thrust is produced. At speeds above trolling, the boat may rapidly lose steering response and may continue straight even if the wheel is turned. Steering response returns when you apply throttle again, or when the boat slows to trolling speed where idle thrust can turn it gradually.

<PIC>

If you notice any unusual looseness, delayed response, binding, uneven steering, or the boat does not turn as expected, reduce speed carefully, keep clear of traffic and obstacles, and do not continue normal operation until the steering system has been inspected and corrected by a qualified Boat Dealer.,['Manual09_206', 'Manual09_207', 'Manual09_239', 'Manual09_240', 'Manual09_241', 'Manual09_242']

At least one fire extinguisher must be aboard and full; two fire extinguishers are recommended. The boat must have one 5-B (B-1) type hand-held portable fire extinguisher when navigating waters controlled by the U.S. Coast Guard.

A fire extinguisher is not standard equipment with this boat. If you do not have one, contact your local Boat Dealer or fire extinguisher dealer for one meeting the proper specifications.

One fire extinguisher is to be mounted in the lockable storage compartment. The fire extinguisher recommended for this location is a chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of two pounds or more.

<PIC>

If you choose to have two fire extinguishers, the other fire extinguisher is to be mounted in the battery compartment. There is a location label outside the compartment. This fire extinguisher located near the engine compartment should be a “clean agent” type designed to displace oxygen, with an inert gas, such as CO2, or FE-36™, and have a capacity of five pounds or more.

<PIC>

The location label indicates that a fire extinguisher is located inside a compartment.

<PIC>,['Manual09_211', 'Manual09_212', 'Boat_04']

To care for the boat’s throttle cable, keep the moving cable parts clean, protected, and properly lubricated with water-resistant marine grease. This helps the remote control levers move smoothly and helps prevent corrosion or sticking, especially after saltwater use or long-term storage.

Recommended care steps:

1. Make sure the boat is safely shut down
- Turn off the engines before doing any maintenance.
- Remove the main switch keys and make sure the remote control levers are not being moved while you work.
- Allow the engine area to cool if the boat was recently operated.

2. Locate the throttle-cable grease point
- The throttle-cable inner wires should be greased at the pulley wheel of the APS area.
- The image below shows the correct lubrication points. Apply grease where the cable inner wire runs around/near the pulley wheel.

<PIC>

3. Apply water-resistant grease
- Use a suitable water-resistant marine grease.
- Apply a light, even coating to the exposed throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel.
- Do not over-pack the area with grease; too much grease can attract dirt and debris.

4. Check cable movement
- After greasing, move the remote control levers gently through their range to help distribute the grease.
- The levers should move smoothly without binding, sticking, or unusual resistance.
- If the throttle feels stiff, does not return properly, or the engine response feels uneven, have the throttle cable and linkage inspected by a qualified boat technician.

5. Add it to your regular care routine
- Lubricate the throttle-cable inner wires during long-term storage preparation and whenever the cable area looks dry or exposed to corrosion.
- If you operate often in saltwater, rinse the boat properly after use and check lubrication points more frequently.

Important: Do not force a stiff throttle lever. A sticky or damaged throttle cable can affect control of the boat, so it should be inspected and corrected before operating again.,['Manual09_263']

To charge the camera battery when you are not using the camera:

1. Remove the battery from the camera and reattach the protective cover to prevent shorting.

<PIC>

2. Attach the battery to the charger.

For CG-580: Flip out the battery charger’s prongs, then insert the prongs into a power outlet. Recharging starts automatically.

<PIC>

For CB-5L: Connect the power cord to the charger and insert the plug into the power outlet. Recharging starts automatically and the red lamp starts blinking.

<PIC>

The red lamp indicates the recharge level: it blinks while charging and stays lit when the charge reaches 90% or higher.

<PIC>

After the red lamp lights, continue to recharge the battery for an hour to attain a full charge. After recharging, detach the battery and unplug the power cord from the power outlet.

If the camera will not be used for a prolonged period, remove the battery and attach the protective cover. Before using the camera again, be sure to recharge the battery.,['Manual10_10', 'Manual10_12', 'Manual10_13', 'Manual10_14']

If the camera battery is exhausted or the camera has powered off, you can replace it with a fully charged battery as follows:

1. Turn the camera power switch to <OFF> before opening the battery compartment. This helps prevent interruption or possible data-writing issues.

2. Open the battery compartment cover. Slide the compartment lever in the direction of the arrow, then lift the cover open.
<PIC>

3. Insert a fully charged compatible battery pack. Use a BP-511A battery pack, or another compatible pack such as BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512. Hold the battery with the electrical contacts facing downward, then push it into the battery compartment until it locks firmly in place. Do not force it in the opposite direction, as reversed polarity may damage the battery or camera.
<PIC>

4. Close the battery compartment cover. Press the cover down until it clicks or snaps shut. If the cover is not fully closed, the camera may not operate even after a charged battery is installed.
<PIC>

After installation, turn the power switch back to <ON> and check the battery level on the camera display. If the battery icon still shows low or the camera does not turn on, recharge the battery fully or try another compatible battery. If the battery drains very quickly even after charging, it may have reached the end of its service life and should be replaced.,['Manual10_16', 'Camera_09', 'Camera_10']

To power the camera from a household power socket, use the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2. This kit includes the AC adapter, DC Coupler, and power cord, and it lets you use the camera without relying on the battery level.

Before connecting, make sure the camera power switch is set to OFF.

1. Connect the DC Coupler to the AC adapter

Insert the DC Coupler plug firmly into the socket on the AC adapter.

<PIC>

2. Connect the power cord and plug it into the wall outlet

Connect the power cord to the AC adapter first, then insert the power plug fully into a household power outlet.

<PIC>

3. Place the cord into the groove

Carefully fit the cord into the groove so it sits properly and is not pinched, bent sharply, or damaged.

<PIC>

4. Insert the DC Coupler into the camera

Open the battery compartment cover, then open the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler into the battery compartment until it locks into position. Route the cord through the notch, then close the battery compartment cover.

<PIC>

After everything is connected, turn the camera on and use it normally.

Important safety notes:
- Use only the specified ACK-E2 AC Adapter Kit for this camera.
- Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera is switched on.
- Always insert the power plug fully into the outlet.
- Do not handle the plug with wet hands.
- When unplugging, hold the plug itself rather than pulling on the cord.
- Keep the cord away from heat sources, and do not bend, twist, cut, or place heavy objects on it.
- When you are finished using the camera, turn the camera off and unplug the power cord from the outlet.,['Camera_14', 'Camera_15', 'Manual10_19', 'Manual10_20']

To mount the camera lens before shooting, follow these steps:

1. Turn the camera off and prepare the lens/body
   - Hold the camera with the lens mount facing slightly downward to help prevent dust from entering.
   - Avoid touching the camera’s electrical contacts, the rear lens contacts, mirror area, or the inside of the lens mount.
   - Remove the rear lens cap from the lens and the body cap from the camera by turning them in the direction indicated.

<PIC>

2. Align the lens with the correct mount index
   - For an EF-S lens: align the lens with the white EF-S lens mount index on the camera.
   - For an EF lens: align the lens with the red EF lens mount index on the camera.
   - Insert the lens straight into the mount, then turn it in the direction shown until you hear or feel a click. The click means the lens is locked in place.

<PIC>

3. Set the focus mode and remove the front cap
   - On the lens, set the focus mode switch to AF if you want to use autofocus.
   - If the switch is set to MF, autofocus will not operate, and you will need to focus manually.
   - Finally, squeeze and remove the front lens cap before taking photos.

<PIC>

After mounting, gently check that the lens is securely attached and does not rotate loosely. If you are not taking photos immediately, keep the front lens cap on to protect the lens surface.,['Manual10_21', 'Manual10_22', 'Manual10_23']

Before taking photos, install the CF card as follows:

1. Make sure the camera is ready for card insertion.  
   For safety, turn the camera off first, and avoid opening the card slot cover while the access lamp is lit or blinking.

2. Open the CF card slot cover.  
   Slide the cover in the direction of the arrow, then swing it open as shown.

<PIC>

3. Insert the CF card in the correct direction.  
   Hold the card with the label side facing toward you. Insert the end with the small holes into the camera first, following the direction shown by the arrow. Push it in gently until it is fully seated. The CF card eject button will pop out when the card is inserted correctly.

   Important: Do not force the card. If it does not slide in smoothly, remove it and check the direction again, because inserting it the wrong way may damage the camera or the card.

<PIC>

4. Close the CF card slot cover.  
   Swing the cover closed, then slide it in the direction of the arrow until it clicks securely into place. The camera may not operate properly if the card slot cover is not fully closed.

<PIC>

5. Confirm the card is recognized.  
   After the card is installed and the camera is turned on, check the LCD panel. The number of remaining shots should be displayed, which indicates that the camera has detected the CF card and is ready for photography.

<PIC>,['Manual10_25', 'Manual10_26', 'Manual10_27', 'Manual10_28']

The camera’s shutter button is not designed to be removed by the user. It is an integrated control on the camera’s top assembly, and attempting to pry it off or disassemble it can damage the button mechanism, weather sealing, internal contacts, or the top cover.

<PIC>

For normal operation, the shutter button works in two stages:
1. Press halfway: activates autofocus and automatic exposure.
2. Press fully: releases the shutter and takes the picture.

<PIC>

If your goal is repair or troubleshooting, here are safer steps to try first:
- If the button feels sticky: turn the camera off, remove the battery, and gently clean around the button edge with a dry, soft brush or air blower. Do not use liquid cleaner directly on the button.
- If the button does not respond: reinsert the battery, make sure the camera is in shooting mode, and try pressing the button halfway first, then fully.
- If the button is physically loose, jammed, cracked, or unresponsive after cleaning: the camera should be inspected by a qualified camera repair technician, as the top control assembly may need servicing.

So, while you can use and clean around the shutter button carefully, removing the shutter button itself is not recommended as a DIY operation.,['Manual10_31', 'Manual10_32']

To replace the camera’s date/time back-up battery, use a new CR2016 lithium battery. This small battery keeps the camera’s date and time stored; it typically lasts about 5 years. If the date/time resets after you replace the main camera battery, the back-up battery likely needs replacing.

Please follow these steps:

1. Turn the camera’s power switch to OFF.

2. Open the battery compartment cover and remove the main battery pack first. This gives you access to the date/time battery holder inside the compartment.

<PIC>

3. Locate the small date/time battery holder, then pull it out carefully.

<PIC>

4. Remove the old CR2016 battery from the holder.

<PIC>

5. Insert the new CR2016 lithium battery into the holder. Make sure the + and - sides are facing the correct direction as shown, otherwise the camera will not retain the date/time properly.

<PIC>

6. Slide the battery holder back into place, reinstall the main battery pack, and close the battery compartment cover securely.

After replacement, the camera’s date/time setting will be reset. Turn the camera back on and set the correct date and time again.

Important: Use only a CR2016 lithium battery for the date/time back-up battery.,['Manual10_46', 'Manual10_47', 'Manual10_48', 'Manual10_49']

To shoot a subject that is not in the center of the frame, use focus lock. This lets you focus on the subject first, then keep that focus while you recompose the picture. Steps: 1. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP. In these modes, you can select the AF point and AF operation more freely. 2. Select the AF point you want to use. The AF point is the small focus mark you see in the viewfinder. Choose one that can be placed over your subject, or use the center AF point and recompose afterward. 3. Focus on the off-center subject. Look through the viewfinder, place the selected AF point over the subject, then press the shutter button halfway. Keep it pressed halfway when focus is achieved. 4. Keep holding the shutter button halfway and recompose. While continuing to press halfway, move the camera to adjust the composition so the subject is positioned where you want in the frame. 5. Take the picture. Press the shutter button fully to shoot. Important notes:
  • Focus lock works only in One-Shot AF, which is best for still subjects.
  • If the camera is set to AI Servo AF, or AI Focus AF has switched to Servo tracking, focus lock will not work because the camera keeps refocusing continuously.
  • For moving subjects, use AI Servo AF instead and keep the AF point on the subject while shooting.
  • Focus lock can also be used in most Basic Zone modes; in that case, simply start by placing the AF point over the subject and pressing the shutter button halfway.
,['Manual10_107']

During self-timer or remote switch operation when your eye does not cover the viewfinder eyepiece, stray light may enter the eyepiece and affect the exposure when the image is captured. In such a case, use the eyepiece cover.

Remove the eyecup. From the bottom of the eyecup, push it upward.
<PIC>

Attaching the Eyepiece Cover. Slide the eyepiece cover down into the eyepiece groove to attach it.
<PIC>,['Manual10_154', 'Manual10_155']

To select the AF Mode before taking a picture, follow these steps:

1. Set the lens focus mode switch to <AF>.

<PIC>

2. Turn the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP.

AF Mode selection is available in these Creative Zone modes. In Basic Zone modes, the camera chooses the AF mode automatically, so you will not be able to manually select it.

3. Press the <AF-WB> button on the top of the camera.

<PIC>

4. While looking at the LCD panel, turn the Main Dial to choose the AF mode you want.

<PIC>

You can select from these AF modes:

- ONE SHOT / One-Shot AF: Best for still subjects. Press the shutter button halfway to focus once; focus locks while you keep the shutter button half-pressed.
- AI FOCUS / AI Focus AF: Best when the subject may start moving. The camera begins like One-Shot AF, then automatically switches to AI Servo AF if it detects movement.
- AI SERVO / AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects. The camera keeps adjusting focus while you hold the shutter button halfway, which is useful for action, sports, pets, or subjects moving toward or away from you.

After the desired AF mode appears on the LCD panel, you can press the shutter button halfway to focus, then take the picture.,['Manual10_99', 'Camera_30', 'Camera_31']

To set the camera to “P” mode, rotate the camera’s Mode Dial until the mark lines up with P. P mode means Program AE mode. In this mode, the camera automatically selects the shutter speed and aperture according to the subject’s brightness, so it is useful for general shooting while still allowing you to adjust more settings than Full Auto mode. After selecting P, press the shutter button halfway to let the camera meter the scene. The shutter speed and aperture value will appear in the viewfinder or on the top LCD panel. If those values do not blink, the exposure is suitable and you can press the shutter button fully to take the picture. If the exposure values blink, the scene may be too dark or too bright. In that case, you can adjust the ISO, use flash for a dark scene, or reduce incoming light for an overly bright scene.,['Manual10_116']

You can view the camera’s captured images on a TV by connecting the camera to the TV’s VIDEO IN terminal with the supplied video cable. This is mainly for playback/reviewing photos already captured, including checking images right after shooting. It does not provide a live TV view for composing the shot while you are shooting.

Please follow these steps:

1. Turn off both the camera and the TV before connecting or disconnecting the cable.

2. Connect the camera to the TV.
- Open the camera’s terminal cover.
- Plug the video cable fully into the camera’s video output terminal.
- Connect the other end of the cable to the TV’s VIDEO IN terminal.
- Make sure both plugs are inserted securely.

<PIC>

3. Turn on the TV and select the correct input source.
- Switch the TV input/line input to Video IN or the corresponding AV input.
- If the TV has multiple video inputs, choose the one where the cable is connected.

4. Turn on the camera, then press the playback button.
- The image will appear on the TV screen.
- The camera’s LCD monitor will not display the image while the TV output is being used.
- You can then use the camera controls to browse through your captured images as usual.

<PIC>

5. When finished:
- Turn the camera power switch to OFF.
- Turn off the TV.
- Then disconnect the video cable.

If the image does not display correctly, or the picture looks distorted/black and white, check the camera’s [Video system] setting and match it to your TV’s video format. Also note that depending on the TV, the edges of the image may be slightly cut off.,['Manual10_182', 'Manual10_183']

You can delete just one photo directly from the camera in playback mode:

1. Press the Playback button to display your photos on the LCD screen. The most recently captured image will appear first.

<PIC>

2. Turn the dial to find the photo you want to delete. Make sure the unwanted photo is the one currently displayed on the screen.

3. Press the Erase button, marked with the trash can icon. An erase menu will appear at the bottom of the screen.

<PIC>

4. Turn the dial to select [Erase], then press SET to confirm. The access lamp will blink while the camera deletes the image.

<PIC>

Important: Once a photo is erased, it cannot be recovered from the camera. If the camera does not allow the image to be deleted, the photo may be protected; cancel the protection first, then try deleting it again. If you want to delete another single image, repeat the same steps for the next photo.,['Manual10_184', 'Manual10_185', 'Manual10_186']

Yes, you can erase all images from the camera’s CF card at once. Please note that this will delete all unprotected images, and erased images cannot be recovered, so only continue if you are sure you do not need them.

1. Press the Playback button to display an image on the LCD screen.

2. Press the Erase button, marked with the trash can icon. The erase options will appear at the bottom of the screen.

3. Turn the dial to highlight [All], then press SET. A confirmation screen will appear asking whether to erase all images.

4. Turn the dial to select [OK], then press SET to confirm.
