During the pressure-cooking process, please follow these safety precautions through all three stages: pre-heating, cooking, and depressurization.

<PIC>

During pre-heating, the cooker is building steam and pressure inside the pot. The float valve will rise once enough pressure has built up, which means the lid is locked. Do not try to open or move the lid at this point.

<PIC>

During cooking, the appliance maintains high or low pressure automatically. Keep hands, face, and exposed skin away from the steam release area, and do not cover or block the steam release valve.

<PIC>

After cooking ends, the cooker can still be very hot and pressurized. Pressure must be released safely before opening the lid.

Key safety rules:

1. Never force the lid open  
Do not attempt to remove the lid while the float valve is up. The contents are under extreme pressure. Wait until the float valve has fully dropped before opening the lid.

2. Keep clear of hot steam  
Steam released from the steam release valve is very hot. Do not place your hands, face, or any exposed skin over the valve when releasing pressure.

3. Do not cover the steam release valve  
The steam release valve must remain unobstructed so pressure can release properly.

4. Use the correct pressure-release method  
Do not use quick release for fatty, oily, thick, or high-starch foods, such as stews, chili, pasta, or congee. Also avoid quick release for foods that expand during cooking, such as beans and grains, as this can cause food or liquid to spray from the valve.

5. Open only when fully depressurized  
Once cooking is complete, allow the cooker to depressurize according to the recipe or selected venting method. Open the lid only after pressure has fully reduced and the float valve is down.,['Manual30_6', 'Manual30_7', 'Manual30_8']

You can adjust the intensity on your electric toothbrush directly from the handle, or customize it in the app.

Your toothbrush has 3 intensity levels, shown by the number of lights on the handle:
- High intensity: three lights — best if you prefer a stronger clean and are already comfortable with sonic brushing.
- Medium intensity: two lights — a balanced option for everyday cleaning.
- Low intensity: one light — a gentler option, ideal if you have sensitive teeth or gums, are new to electric brushing, or want a softer brushing feel.

<PIC>

To change the intensity manually:
1. Locate the intensity indicator area on the toothbrush handle, below the power button.
2. Press the intensity indicator/button to cycle through the available levels.
3. Stop when the number of lights matches the intensity you want.

You can change the intensity before brushing, while brushing, or after brushing.

<PIC>

A helpful tip: if you feel the brushing is too strong or your gums feel sensitive, switch to a lower intensity and use light pressure. The toothbrush is designed to do the cleaning work for you, so you do not need to scrub. If you apply too much pressure for too long, the toothbrush may automatically reduce the intensity by one level to help protect your gums.

You can also adjust and personalize the intensity settings through the app if your toothbrush is paired with your phone.,['toothbrush0_06', 'toothbrush0_07']

You can customize the toothbrush by turning the following features on or off:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Pressure Sensor Feedback
- Scrubbing Feedback
- Brush head replacement reminder

Please note: if Pressure Sensor Feedback is turned off, Adaptive Intensity will also be turned off because Adaptive Intensity depends on pressure detection.

There are two ways to manage these features:

1. Using the app
You can activate or deactivate these features in the app:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Scrubbing Feedback

To set them up in the app:
1. Download and open the app on your phone.
2. Make sure Bluetooth is turned on.
3. Pick up the toothbrush so the handle wakes up and the lights turn on.
4. Follow the guided steps in the app to pair the toothbrush.
5. After pairing, go to the toothbrush settings/customization area in the app.
6. Turn Adaptive Intensity or Scrubbing Feedback on or off according to your preference.

The app can also be used to customize other brushing preferences such as mode and intensity settings.

2. Using the toothbrush handle
You can activate or deactivate these features directly from the handle:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Pressure Sensor Feedback
- Brush head replacement reminder

Step 1: Place the toothbrush handle on the charging stand.

Step 2: Press and hold the power button. The length of time you hold the button determines which feature you are changing.

<PIC>

Use the timing and signals below:

- Adaptive Intensity: hold the power button for up to 3 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, and the SenseIQ indicator will light up for 3 seconds.
- Pressure Sensor Feedback: hold the power button for up to 5 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, then 2 beeps, and the SenseIQ indicator plus the light ring will light up purple for 3 seconds.
- Brush head replacement reminder: hold the power button for up to 7 seconds. You will hear 1 beep, then 2 beeps, then 3 beeps, and the brush head replacement reminder indicator will light up for 3 seconds.

After you release the button, check the battery indicator and tone pattern to confirm whether the feature was turned on or off.

<PIC>

Confirmation signals:

- Activated: the battery indicator blinks white 3 times, and you hear 3 tones going from low to high.
- Deactivated: the battery indicator blinks amber 3 times, and you hear 3 tones going from high to low.

In short:
- Use the app for Adaptive Intensity and Scrubbing Feedback.
- Use the handle on the charging stand for Adaptive Intensity, Pressure Sensor Feedback, and the Brush head replacement reminder.,['Manual37_13', 'toothbrush0_11']

To charge the electric toothbrush while it is inside the charging travel case, please follow these steps:

1. Connect the USB-C cord to the charging port on the travel case, then connect the other end to the USB wall adapter.
<PIC>

2. Plug the USB wall adapter into a working electrical outlet.

3. Place the toothbrush handle into the travel case, making sure it sits correctly in its charging position. If you are storing the brush head as well, place it in its designated slot.
<PIC>

4. Check that charging has started properly. You should notice the following signs:
- The toothbrush beeps twice.
- The handle lights illuminate in an upward motion.
- The battery indicator blinks white while charging.
<PIC>

5. Keep the travel case plugged in until the toothbrush is fully charged. Once charging is complete, the battery light will stop blinking and turn off. For better stability while charging, place the travel case on its side.
<PIC>

If you do not see the white blinking battery indicator or hear the two beeps, please make sure the cable is firmly connected to the travel case and wall adapter, the outlet is working, and the toothbrush handle is seated correctly inside the case.,['Manual37_18', 'Manual37_19', 'Manual37_20', 'Manual37_21']
